title: Container not able to talk to a specific container, other containers are fine
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I've 3 containers running grafana, prometheus and nginx. The nginx container cannot talk to grafana over the host (10.10.0.1:3000) but can talk to prometheus (10.10.0.1:9000) or any other container I spawn. Both prometheus and grafana can talk to each other over the host.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

0. Host runs a wireguard interface with address 10.10.0.1/24

1. run grafana with default settings (and `-p  10.10.0.1:3000:3000`) via podman run, repeat for prometheus or anything else

2. run an nginx image (mine can be found at `alexj9/letsencrypt`), attach to the container

3. in the nginx container `curl http://10.10.0.1:3000`

**Describe the results you received:**

It times out. 

`curl http://10.10.0.1:9000` works as expected and from within each container.

`curl http://10.88.0.77:3000` works as expected.

**Describe the results you expected:**

It connects

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

iptables output:
```
Chain INPUT (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination         
ACCEPT     tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:https ctstate NEW,ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:http ctstate NEW,ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     udp  --  anywhere             anywhere             udp dpt:51234
ACCEPT     udp  --  anywhere             anywhere             udp dpts:60000:61000
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             anywhere             ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:ssh flags:FIN,SYN,RST,ACK/SYN /* accept new ssh connections */ ctstate NEW
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             anywhere            
ACCEPT     icmp --  anywhere             anywhere            
DROP       all  --  anywhere             anywhere            
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             anywhere            

Chain FORWARD (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination         
CNI-FORWARD  all  --  anywhere             anywhere             /* CNI firewall plugin rules */

Chain OUTPUT (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination         
ACCEPT     tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp spt:https ctstate ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp spt:http ctstate ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             anywhere            
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             anywhere            

Chain CNI-ADMIN (1 references)
target     prot opt source               destination         

Chain CNI-FORWARD (1 references)
target     prot opt source               destination         
CNI-ADMIN  all  --  anywhere             anywhere             /* CNI firewall plugin rules */
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             10.88.0.61           ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     all  --  10.88.0.61           anywhere            
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             10.88.0.70           ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             10.88.0.76           ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     all  --  10.88.0.76           anywhere            
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             10.88.0.77           ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     all  --  10.88.0.77           anywhere            

```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:

  git commit: ""

host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'

    version: 'conmon version 2.0.0, commit: unknown'

  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 8589926400
  SwapTotal: 8589926400
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: agony
  kernel: 5.0.0-25-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 15h 13m 19.41s (Approximately 0.62 days)

  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:

  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Compression: lz4
    Parent Dataset: tank/containers
    Parent Quota: "no"
    Space Available: "2892745365632"
    Space Used By Parent: "1035120640"
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical box.

comments.body: can you please make this more generic? like make every container an nginx container and see if this works?  we dont know much about the stuff you're running but it would a stronger argument that there is something wrong with podman.  when you inspect the containers, does the port assignment look correct? does podman port look correct?
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@spaghetti- Since we have had no feedback from August, I am going to close,  If you want us to continue to look at this and want to provide feedback, please reopen.
